heading: Step 4: View the details of the service account.
body:
The details include:

- The obfuscated client secret for the service account

- The date the client secret was last used

- The date the client secret was created

- The IP (Internet Protocol) addresses from which the service account can access the API (Application Programming Interface)

- The [organization role](organization-roles) the service account has been assigned

You can use the Atlas Administration API to [list the
names and details of the service accounts that have access to your
organization](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listserviceaccounts).

To view the details of an API (Application Programming Interface) key in an organization using the Atlas UI, follow these steps:

- Click  to the right of the API (Application Programming Interface) Key.

- Click View Details.

The \<Public Key> API Key Details modal displays:

- The obfuscated Private Key

- The date the Key was last used

- The date the Key was created

- The IP (Internet Protocol) addresses from which the Key can access the API (Application Programming Interface)

- The projects to which the Key has been granted access

You can view a list of API (Application Programming Interface) keys, the details of an API (Application Programming Interface) key,
or the access list for an API (Application Programming Interface) key in an
organization using the Atlas CLI.
